---
title: "1.15 String Manipulation"
subtitle: "Using Objects and Methods"
format:
  clean-revealjs:
    slide-number: true
    css: unit1-shared.css
lang: zh-CN
---

## Course Directory

### Return to the course outline

[← Back to AP CSA / 返回课程目录](../../index.html)

## Strings as Objects

### Text in Java is stored as `String` objects

`String` is a class in the built-in <span class="term">`java.lang`</span> package.

::: {.tight-list}
- strings are <span class="term">reference types</span>, not primitive types
- a string variable can hold a reference or `null`
- class names like `String` start with a capital letter
:::

![Initial string reference](../../assets/1-using-objects-and-methods/1-15-string-manipulation/greeting.png){fig-align="center" width="18%"}

## Creating and Referring to Strings

### Literal form and constructor form

```java
String greeting1 = "Hello";
String greeting2 = new String("Welcome");
```

Both statements create a `String` object and store a reference in the variable.

![String object and class relationship](../../assets/1-using-objects-and-methods/1-15-string-manipulation/stringObject.png){fig-align="center" width="62%"}

## Concatenation and Immutability

### Building new strings with `+`

```java
String start = "Happy Birthday";
String name = "Jose";
String result = start + " " + name;
result += "!";
```

::: {.tight-list}
- `+` and `+=` can concatenate strings
- Java converts other values to strings when needed
- strings are <span class="term">immutable</span>
- concatenation creates a <span class="mark">new string</span>; it does not modify the old one
:::

## Index and Length

### String positions start at 0

![String indices](../../assets/1-using-objects-and-methods/1-15-string-manipulation/stringIndicies.png){fig-align="center" width="62%"}

::: {.tight-list}
- the first character is at index `0`
- the last character is at index `length() - 1`
- an invalid index causes an <span class="term">IndexOutOfBoundsException</span>
:::

```java
String word = "Code";
int size = word.length();
```

## Common String Methods

### `length`, `substring`, and `indexOf`

```java
String s = "baby";
System.out.println(s.length());      // 4
System.out.println(s.substring(2));  // by
System.out.println(s.indexOf("b"));  // 0
```

::: {.tight-list}
- `length()` returns the number of characters
- `substring(begin)` copies from `begin` to the end
- `substring(begin, end)` stops <span class="mark">before</span> `end`
- `indexOf(...)` finds a character or string position, or returns `-1`
:::

## Comparing Strings

### Use methods, not `==`

::: {.two-col}
![compareTo behavior](../../assets/1-using-objects-and-methods/1-15-string-manipulation/compareTo.png){width="100%"}
![Pig Latin challenge icon](../../assets/1-using-objects-and-methods/1-15-string-manipulation/pig.png){width="42%"}
:::

```java
message.equals("Hello!");
message.compareTo("Zoo");
```

::: {.tight-list}
- `equals(...)` returns `true` or `false`
- `compareTo(...)` returns `0`, a negative number, or a positive number
- both are <span class="mark">case-sensitive</span>
- `==` checks whether references are the same object, not whether characters match
:::

## Common Mistakes with Strings

### The usual errors are predictable

::: {.tight-list}
- forgetting spaces during concatenation
- thinking `substring(begin, end)` includes the character at `end`
- expecting `toLowerCase()` or similar calls to change the original string automatically
- using `==` instead of `equals(...)`
- calling string methods on a `null` reference
:::

## Classroom Tasks

### Practice worth keeping

Retained classroom work for this topic:

::: {.tight-list}
- string-reference output checks
- concatenation and indexing quick checks
- `substring`, `length`, and `indexOf` tracing
- compareTo / equals comparison tasks
- <span class="term">1.15.8 Coding Challenge: Pig Latin</span>
:::

## Classroom Check

### A complete answer should...

::: {.tight-list}
- identify `String` as a class type in `java.lang`
- explain concatenation and string immutability
- use `length`, `substring`, and `indexOf` correctly
- apply zero-based indexing to strings
- compare strings with `equals` or `compareTo`, not `==`
:::

## End

### Return to the course outline

[← Back to AP CSA / 返回课程目录](../../index.html)
